domains.name: Domain 1: Monitoring, Logging, Analysis, Remediation, and Performance Optimization

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to collect metrics and logs from EC2 and AWS services?
questions.explanation: CloudWatch is the primary monitoring service.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to run operational tasks like patching at scale?
questions.explanation: Systems Manager automates fleet operations.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to aggregate logs across accounts?

options.text: CloudWatch cross-account observability or Centralized logging

questions.explanation: Observability links accounts for unified views.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to trace API calls for troubleshooting?
questions.explanation: CloudTrail records API activity.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to capture VPC traffic metadata?

options.text: Packet sniff all prod without policy

questions.explanation: Flow Logs capture IP traffic metadata.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to create dashboards for golden signals?
questions.explanation: Dashboards centralize operational metrics.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to use synthetic canaries to monitor endpoints?
questions.explanation: Canaries run scheduled tests against URLs.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to store configuration compliance history?
questions.explanation: Config tracks resource configuration over time.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to automate backups across services?
questions.explanation: AWS Backup applies plans to multiple resources.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to replicate servers to AWS for DR with minimal downtime?
questions.explanation: Elastic DR replicates and orchestrates failover.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to maintain RDS backups and point-in-time recovery?
questions.explanation: RDS backups enable PITR within retention.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to health check and failover DNS?

options.text: Route 53 health checks and failover routing

questions.explanation: Route 53 routes away from unhealthy endpoints.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to scale EC2 automatically on CPU?
questions.explanation: Auto Scaling adjusts capacity from metrics.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to distribute traffic for HA?
questions.explanation: ELB spans AZs for resilience.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to restore objects deleted from S3 accidentally?
questions.explanation: Versioning retains prior object versions.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to test DR with game days using isolated stacks?

options.text: CloudFormation stack sets in DR Region

questions.explanation: Regular DR exercises validate RTO/RPO.

questions.text: Which are AWS services used for encryption or key management? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: KMS manages keys; Secrets Manager stores and rotates secrets.

questions.text: Which TWO practices align with the AWS Well-Architected Framework? (Select TWO.)

options.text: Design for failure and automate recovery
Use single-AZ for all production data stores

questions.explanation: Reliability and operational excellence emphasize automation, testing, and learning from failure.

questions.text: Which TWO official AWS resources help you prepare for AWS Certified CloudOps Engineer – Associate? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: Use official AWS exam guides, Skill Builder, and AWS documentation—not real exam content.

questions.text: According to AWS best practices documented in 'Amazon CloudWatch', what should you do FIRST when addressing Monitoring, Logging, Analysis, Remediation, and Performance Optimization objectives?

options.text: Review the official AWS documentation and align designs to the exam guide domains

Use root user credentials for daily operations
